controls.rationale: Centralize human AWS access with MFA, role/group assignment, review, and auditability.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: No repository-managed implementation exists for IAM Identity Center access governance.
controls.gapSummary: The repo does not define IAM Identity Center permission sets, groups, assignments, or account access boundaries; human AWS access remains externally and manually governed.

evidence.summary: Bootstrap IAM roles are codified, but IAM Identity Center resources are absent.
Architecture report identifies security improvements for production readiness.

controls.title: Human AWS access is not yet codified
controls.objective: Centralize human AWS access with MFA, role/group assignment, review, and auditability.

controls.rationale: Make Kubernetes API ownership explicit and versioned instead of relying on creator privileges or temporary admin users.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: The GitHub apply role has permissions to create EKS access entries; if GitHub creates the cluster it can become the consistent cluster owner. Terraform/GitHub apply can manage the platform through the EKS API.
controls.gapSummary: Terraform does not yet declare named operator access entries, aws-auth mappings, Identity Center role mappings, or Kubernetes RBAC for human operators.

evidence.summary: EKS cluster module defines cluster access mode and access-related settings.
Apply role includes permissions needed for EKS access entry management.
Runbook captures EKS access troubleshooting context.

controls.title: EKS operator access entries are incomplete
controls.objective: Make Kubernetes API ownership explicit and versioned instead of relying on creator privileges or temporary admin users.

controls.rationale: Limit the maximum permissions of automation and runtime roles even when Terraform creates or updates IAM identities.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: GitHub OIDC roles, EKS cluster and node roles, and controller IRSA roles apply the lab-devops-permissions-boundary. IAM roles and policies are Terraform-managed and visible in PR plans.
controls.gapSummary: The boundary policy itself is managed outside this repository, and policy-as-code does not yet automatically block overly broad permissions or wildcard pass-role exceptions.

resources.name: GitHub, EKS, node, and controller roles

evidence.summary: Bootstrap roles attach the configured permissions boundary.
EKS roles are configured with a permission boundary.

controls.title: Permission boundary constrains project IAM roles
controls.objective: Limit the maximum permissions of automation and runtime roles even when Terraform creates or updates IAM identities.
